get_pull_request: Returns pull request metadata including title, body, state, author, head/base branches, and merge readiness flags. Does not include code diffs, changed files, or review comments.
get_pull_request_diff: Returns the raw unified diff showing all line-level additions and deletions across every file changed in the pull request. Provides detailed code changes, unlike tools that only list files or metadata.
get_pull_request_files: Returns a JSON string with a stub response containing the tool identifier and pull request number.
get_pull_request_reviews: Returns top-level review decisions for a pull request, including author, state (APPROVED / CHANGES_REQUESTED / COMMENTED), and body text. Does not include inline per-line comments.
get_pull_request_comments: Returns inline per-line comments on a pull request's diff. Unlike get_pull_request_reviews, this tool returns per-line inline comments, not top-level review decisions.
merge_pull_request: Merges a pull request into its base branch using a specified merge method (merge, squash, or rebase), irreversibly modifying the target branch's commit history as a write operation.
search_repositories: Searches GitHub repository metadata including name, description, topics, and README content, returning records with star count, language, and owner. Does not search file contents.
search_code: Searches GitHub repository file contents for the query string, returning file paths and matching code snippets with surrounding context. Does not search repository metadata or issue text.
search_issues: Searches issues and pull requests across repositories, returning records with state, labels, and assignees. Does not search repository metadata or source file contents.
search_users: Searches GitHub users and organizations for matching profiles, returning a stub response.
get_file_contents: Returns the decoded text content of a file or directory listing, and is read-only — does not modify the repository.
create_or_update_file: Creates or updates a file in a GitHub repository, writing a new commit to the target branch. Requires content and a commit message, with an optional existing SHA for updates.
push_files: Pushes multiple files to a GitHub repository in a single commit, writing to the repository and creating the branch if it does not exist. Returns the file count.
list_pull_requests: Returns pull request records for a repository, filtered by state, branch, and sort parameters. Unlike list_issues, this tool returns pull request records only — not issues or commits.
list_issues: Lists and filters repository issues, returning only issue records — not pull requests, even though pull requests are implemented as issues internally in GitHub's data model. Returns a JSON string with a stub response.
list_commits: Returns a JSON stub with tool identifier and purpose.
list_branches: Returns branch names and their head commit SHAs for a GitHub repository, optionally filtered to protected branches. Includes commit metadata for each branch, unlike tools that return standalone commits, issues, or pull requests.
get_repository: Returns metadata for a GitHub repository including owner, name, description, visibility, default branch, fork status, star count, open issue count, and clone URLs in a JSON string. Read-only — does not modify the repository.
list_repositories: Returns a JSON string with a stub indicating repository listing for a GitHub user.
create_repository: Creates a new GitHub repository under the authenticated user or organization, creating a permanent repository that persists until explicitly deleted.
fork_repository: Forks an existing GitHub repository into the authenticated user's account or specified organization, optionally renaming it. CREATES a permanent, independent copy with lasting side-effects.
